text.title: C# Background Services Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns observed in production .NET deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: `Task.Run(async () => { while (true) { ...; await Task.Delay(5000); } })` started from `Program.cs`. On graceful shutdown the loop never exits and the host hangs for 30s before SIGKILL.
antipattern.root-cause: Bypasses `IHost` lifecycle. There is no `stoppingToken`, no DI scope, no health-check hook.
antipattern.fix: Replace with a class extending `BackgroundService`. Inject `IServiceProvider` + channel; resolve scoped deps via `CreateScope()`; propagate `stoppingToken`.

antipattern.symptom: `Channel.CreateUnbounded<T>()` queue grows without bound under producer spikes; container OOM-killed.
antipattern.root-cause: Unbounded queues offer no back-pressure; producers run unchecked.
antipattern.fix: Switch to `Channel.CreateBounded<T>(new BoundedChannelOptions(capacity) { FullMode = BoundedChannelFullMode.Wait })`. Pick a capacity tied to the worker's drain rate.

antipattern.symptom: `DbContext` (or `IDbContextFactory<TContext>`) injected in the worker constructor. EF Core throws `InvalidOperationException: A second operation was started on this context` under load.

root-cause.source: EF Core docs ŌĆö DbContext lifetime

antipattern.root-cause: `BackgroundService` is a singleton; the captured `DbContext` is reused across items, breaking EF's per-request session model.
antipattern.fix: Constructor takes `IServiceProvider`. Inside the loop: `using var scope = _sp.CreateScope(); var db = scope.ServiceProvider.GetRequiredService<TContext>();`.

antipattern.symptom: `await Task.Delay(5000)` without `stoppingToken`. Pod takes 30s to terminate during rolling deploys; readiness probe failures cascade.
antipattern.root-cause: Missing token = nothing to observe cancellation = `ExecuteAsync` only exits on `ShutdownTimeout`.
antipattern.fix: Pass `stoppingToken` to every async API: `await Task.Delay(period, stoppingToken)`, `await channel.Reader.ReadAllAsync(stoppingToken)`, scope-resolved service calls.
